title: Word Ladder
description: Given two words beginWord and endWord, and a dictionary of words, find the length of the shortest transformation sequence from beginWord to endWord. A transformation sequence is formed by changing exactly one letter at a time such that each transformed word must exist in the dictionary. Note that you cannot transform the beginWord into itself.

examples.explanation: The shortest transformation sequence is: hit -> hot -> dot -> dog -> cog. This sequence has a length of 5.
Since endWord is not in the wordList, no transformation sequence is possible, hence the output is 0.
The shortest transformation sequence is: a -> b -> c. Thus, the length of the sequence is 2.

constraints: All words have the same length.
All words contain only lowercase alphabetic characters.
The beginWord and endWord are not the same.
hints: Consider using breadth-first search (BFS) to explore all possible transformations.
You can represent the words as nodes and their transformations as edges in a graph.
Keep track of visited words to avoid cycles in your search.
Think about how to efficiently generate all possible transformations of a given word.
